articles.title: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,400 on banking, realty gains
articles.excerpt:
Equity benchmarks ended higher for the fourth straight day on Monday, led by strong buying in blue-chip banking, realty and defence counters amid subdued crude oil prices, easing geopolitical tensions and growing expectations of a less hawkish stance from the USFederal Reserve. Asian and European markets showcased mixed trends.

“The calmness in the West Asia region and hopes for a relatively steady corporate earnings season kept investor mood optimistic, although overall sentiment remains cautious,” Ankur Punj, MD & Business Head at Equirus Wealth, said.

According to Vinod Nair, Head of Research at Geojit Investments, continued softness in crude prices would support inflation, the current
articles.citation: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,400 on banking, realty gains — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-06. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/sensex-jumps-521-points-nifty-closes-above-24400-on-banking-realty-gains/article71188985.ece

articles.title: IndusInd Bank among 6 stocks to hit 52-week highs & rally up to 25% in a month
articles.excerpt:
On Monday, the benchmark Sensex surged 521 points to settle at 78,285. Amid the broad-based market rally, six stocks from the BSE 100 Index hit their respective 52-week highs, reaching their highest levels in the past year. A stock touching a 52-week high is generally considered a bullish technical signal, reflecting strong investor confidence, sustained buying momentum, and the potential for further upside in the near term.

New 52-week high: Rs 1857.75| CMP: Rs 1851.1. In the last one month stock has gained about 26%.

New 52-week high: Rs 1012| CMP: Rs 1009.9. In the last one month stock has gained about 12%.

articles.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,430
articles.excerpt:
Bull and Bear Stock Market Prices Concept. 3d Render | Photo Credit: asbe

Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Indian benchmark indices closed higher on Monday, buoyed by a sharp rally in HDFC Bank after its strong June-quarter business update. Lower crude oil prices and easing tensions in the Middle East also boosted investor sentiment.

The Sensex climbed 521.16 points (0.67%) to finish at 78,285.07, while the Nifty advanced 159.50 points (0.66%) to 24,430.35. Additional optimism stemmed from a narrowing monsoon rainfall deficit and indications that foreign investors are reconsidering their India exposure after months of outflows.

articles.title: Britain's FTSE indexes slip as miners offset financial gains
articles.excerpt:
London's FTSE 100 saw a slight dip as precious metal miners declined, countered by gains in financial stocks. EasyJet surged on a sweetened takeover bid, while ITV advanced after agreeing to sell its media division. Investors are also watching for potential boosts to the UK government bond market from the Bank of England, amidst ongoing geopolitical considerations.

articles.title: Wall Street: S&P 500, Nasdaq surge as chip stocks rebound, Broadcom jumps 5.7%, Micron adds 2.8%
articles.excerpt:
The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq surged on Monday, as chip stocks rebounded, while investors focused on second quarter earnings.

At 10:18 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 149.02 points, or 0.28%, to 52,751.05, the S&P 500 gained 38.57 points, or 0.52%, to 7,521.81, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 283.78 points, or 1.10%, to 26,116.45.

As of 9:35 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 rose 0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.8%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up or 0.2%.

Investors are now turning their attention to the minutes of the US Federal Reserve's latest monetary policy meeting, scheduled for release on Wednesday, for fresh clues on the central bank's interest rate outlook
articles.citation: Wall Street: S&P 500, Nasdaq surge as chip stocks rebound, Broadcom jumps 5.7%, Micron adds 2.8% — Mint Markets, 2026-07-06. https://www.livemint.com/market/stock-market-news/wall-street-edges-higher-as-chip-stocks-rebound-11783345550088.html

articles.title: Trump rings Wall Street's opening bells as he ties his presidency to stock market gains
articles.excerpt:
President Trump symbolically opened the stock market from the Oval Office, linking his presidency to market gains amid rising inflation. Despite claims of record-setting markets, only 33% approve of his economic leadership. The initiative promotes "Trump Accounts" for children's investments, highlighting that many families lack direct stock exposure, a benefit often seen in wealthier households. Inflation, however, continues to impact public approval.

articles.title: HDFC Bank shares jump 3.6% on strong Q1FY27 business update
articles.excerpt:
Shares of HDFC Bank surged 3.6 per cent on Monday, leading the Nifty 50 gainers after the lender reported robust growth in deposits and advances for the first quarter of FY27 in its provisional business update.

The stock ended 3.60 per cent positive on the NSE at ₹829.85, hitting a high of ₹831.50.

The bank’s gross advances rose 15.4 per cent y-o-y, while deposits increased 14.7 per cent y-o-y.

According to the bank’s regulatory filing, period-end deposits stood at about ₹31,70,500 crore as of June 30, 2026, compared with ₹27,64,100 crore a year earlier, reflecting a growth of around 14.7 per cent y-o-y.

Bernstein retained its outperform rating with a target price of ₹1,150. The brokerag
articles.citation: HDFC Bank shares jump 3.6% on strong Q1FY27 business update — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-06. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/hdfc-bank-shares-jump-3-on-strong-q1fy27-business-update/article71187981.ece

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 6 July: Swan Corp, Zen Tech, HFCL, HDFC Bank, Thermax, Hitachi Energy among top gainers
articles.excerpt:
Indian stock markets ended in the green in Monday's trade, 6 July, as both benchmark indices closed with gains of over 0.60%, largely driven by HDFC Bank and Axis Bank, while auto and metal stocks also lent strong support, extending Dalal Street's winning streak to four straight sessions.

The Nifty 50 ended the session 0.62% higher at 24,420, its highest closing level in two months. The Sensex also advanced 0.61% to settle at 78,236. The broader markets maintained their winning momentum as well, with the Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 indices gaining 0.40% and 0.70%, respectively.

Among the sectoral indices, Nifty Realty continued to lead the gains, rising another 2%, while Nifty

articles.title: HDFC Bank vs Yes Bank vs Kotak Bank vs IndusInd Bank - Which banking stock to buy after Q1 business update?
articles.excerpt:
Q1 FY27 business update: Banking stocks like HDFC Bank, Yes Bank, Kotak Bank and IndusInd Bank reported their business updates for the quarter ending on 30 June, 2026, last week.

Shares of HDFC Bank and Axis Bank gained as much as 2.5% on Monday, while Kotak Mahindra Bank declined up to 3.5%, after the leading private-sector lenders released their business updates for the April–June quarter ahead of their earnings announcements.

HDFC Bank reported a 15.4% year-on-year (YoY) rise in gross advances to ₹30.61 lakh crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, compared with ₹26.53 lakh crore in the corresponding quarter last year.

Advances under management stood at around ₹31.27 lakh crore at th

articles.title: Global Market: Japan's Topix rises on oil price drop, Nikkei weighed down by tech
articles.excerpt:
Japanese stocks saw a positive start to the week, with the Topix index extending its winning streak to six sessions, buoyed by falling oil prices and a strong global market. While the tech-heavy Nikkei 225 remained flat, sectors like shipping and autos surged. Investors are closely watching central bank policies and currency movements, with the yen weakening and government bonds experiencing a sharp decline.

articles.title: Ather Energy Is said to plan $200 million India share sale
articles.excerpt:
Ather Energy Ltd. is planning a share sale to institutional investors as early as next week to raise as much as $200 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

The electric two-wheeler maker has appointed HSBC Holdings Plc, Axis Capital Ltd. and Nomura Holdings Inc. to manage the qualified institutional placement, the people said, asking not to be identified because the information is private.

Representatives for Ather and the banks didn’t immediately respond to requests for comment.

Shares of Ather have surged more than 250% from their initial public offering price in May 2025, with the gains accelerating since the Iran war increased demand for electric transport options. The
articles.citation: Ather Energy Is said to plan $200 million India share sale — Mint Markets, 2026-07-06. https://www.livemint.com/market/ipo/ather-energy-is-said-to-plan-200-million-india-share-sale-11783339172273.html

articles.title: Nomura names M&M, Hyundai, Ather Energy as top picks after June auto sales; says EV penetration at inflection point
articles.excerpt:
Nomura remains positive on India's automobile sector after strong June sales, saying electric vehicle adoption has reached an inflection point. The brokerage expects continued momentum driven by new launches and improving charging infrastructure, while naming Mahindra & Mahindra, Hyundai Motor India, Ather Energy and Sona BLW among its top picks.
